info.title: Freebox OS API
info.summary: Local API of the Freebox Server gateway.
info.description: Generated from the official Freebox OS documentation (https://dev.freebox.fr/sdk/os/). Paths are version-stripped; the server prepends `/api/v{major}` from live discovery. Every reply is wrapped in an APIResponse envelope which the server unwraps to `result`.

Requires the `settings` permission.

Error codes: `inval`, `exist`, `nospc`, `nodev`, `noent`, `busy`, `inval_band`, `inval_ssid`, `inval_freq`, `inval_cipher`, `inval_key_len`, `inval_key`, `inval_ht_mode`, `inval_ht_needs_wmm`, `inval_ac_needs_ht`, `inval_ac_not_2d4g`

200.description: Success. Body is the unwrapped `result` of the Freebox APIResponse.

error_codes.description: invalid configuration 802.11ac is not supported on 2.4G band

APIResponse.description: The API response is always a JSON object using utf8 encoding.

request_id.description: if you specifiy a request_id in your request, it will be added in the corresponding reply, so that you can correlate responses to the request

WebSocketRequest.description: The requests use the following format:

request_id.description: if you set a request_id in your WebSocketRequest, the same request_id will be returned in the associated response

action.description: the action specified in the associated WebSocketRequest

success.description: indicates if the request was successful

error_code.description: In case of request error, this error_code provides information about the error.

msg.description: In cas of error, provides a French error message relative to the error

WebSocketResponse.description: Responses to such requests will have the following format:

action.description: The action will have the value ‘notification’

source.description: The name of the source of the notification

event.description: The name of event that generated the notification

result.description: the content of the notification (may be omitted if no data has to be transferred along with the notification)

WebSocketNotification.description: When the Freebox wants to send a notification on WebSocket it will have the following format:

password.description: If not empty, the client will have to enter a password to be able to use this airmedia server

AirMediaConfig.description: AirMedia config has the following attributes:

password_protected.description: Is set to true the receiver is protected by a password

capabilities.description: List of receiver capabilities from the following list — Values: `photo`: can display photos; `audio`: can play audio files; `video`: can play video files; `screen`: can display remote screen

AirMediaReceiver.description: AirMedia receivers have the following attributes

action.description: Values: `start`: start playing a media; `stop`: stop playing a media

media_type.description: Values: `photo`: display a photo; `video`: display a video

type.description: The valid call types are: — Values: `missed`: Missed incoming call; `accepted`: Incoming call; `outgoing`: Outgoing call

datetime.description: Call creation timestamp. — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

number.description: Callee number for outgoing calls. Caller number for incoming calls.

name.description: Callee name for outgoing calls. Caller name for incoming calls.

new.description: Call entry as not been acknowledged yet.

contact_id.description: If the number matches an entry in the contact database, the id of the matching contact.

CallEntry.description: Call entries have the following properties

state.description: Values: `going_up`: connection is initializing; `up`: connection is active; `going_down`: connection is about to become inactive; `down`: connection is inactive

bytes_up.description: total uploaded bytes since last connection

bytes_down.description: total downloaded bytes since last connection

ipv4_port_range.description: Some customers share the same IPv4 and each customer is then assigned a port range. The first value is the first port of the assigned range and the second value is the last port (inclusive).

ping.description: should the Freebox respond to external ping requests

is_secure_pass.description: is the admin password secure enough to enable remote access

remote_access_port.description: port number to use for remote HTTP access

remote_access_min_port.description: This field indicate the minimum possible value for remote_access_port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

remote_access_max_port.description: This field indicate the maximum possible value for remote_access_port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

remote_access_ip.description: IPv4 to use for remote access (can be missing if connection is down)

api_remote_access.description: is remote access enabled for apps, or share link

adblock_not_set.description: if set to true adblock setting has never been set by the user

allow_token_request.description: if false, user has disabled new token request. New apps can’t request a new token. Apps that already have a token are still allowed

next_hop.description: the next hop for the prefix

status.description: Values: `down`: unsynchronized; `training`: synchronizing step 1/4; `started`: synchronizing step 2/4; `chan_analysis`: synchronizing step 3/4; `msg_exchange`: synchronizing step 4/4; `showtime`: Ready; `disabled`: Disabled

protocol.description: Values: `t1413`: T1.413; `adsl1_a`: ADSL; `adsl2_a`: ADSL2; `adsl2plus_a`: ADSL2+; `readsl2`: ReachDSL; `adsl2_m`: ADSL2 annex M; `adsl2plus_m`: ADSL2+ annex M; `unknown`: Unknown

rxmt.description: only available when phyr is on

rxmt_corr.description: only available when phyr is on

rxmt_uncorr.description: only available when phyr is on

rtx_tx.description: only available when ginp is on

rtx_c.description: only available when ginp is on

rtx_uc.description: only available when ginp is on

status.description: Values: `disabled`: Disabled; `ok`: Ok; `wait`: Updating; `reqfail`: Request failed; `authfail`: Authentication error; `nocredential`: Invalid credential; `ipinval`: Invalid IP; `hostinval`: Invalid hostname; `abuse`: Blocked because of abuse; `dnserror`: DNS error; `unavailable`: Service unavailable; `nowan`: Unable to get wan IP; `unknown`: Unknown

hostname.description: dns name to use to register

last_update.description: contact last modification timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

ContactEntry.description: Contact entries have the following properties

type.description: Type of number — Values: `fixed`: fixed phone; `mobile`: mobile phone; `work`: work; `fax`: fax; `other`: other

is_default.description: is this number the preferred contact phone number

is_own.description: is this number the Freebox owner number

ContactNumber.description: Contact number have the following properties

type.description: Type of email — Values: `home`: home address; `work`: work address; `other`: other

ContactAddress.description: Contact address have the following properties

type.description: Type of URL — Values: `profile`: profile address; `blog`: blog address; `site`: website address; `other`: other

ContactUrl.description: Contact URL have the following properties

type.description: Type of address — Values: `home`: home address; `work`: work address; `other`: other

ContactEmail.description: Contact email have the following properties

sticky_assign.description: Always assign the same IP to a given host

dns.description: List of dns servers to include in DHCP reply

DhcpConfig.description: DHCP config has the following attributes:

ip.description: IPv4 to assign to the host

host.description: LAN host information from LAN browser (refer to LanHost documentation)

lease_remaining.description: time left before lease needs to be refreshed

assign_time.description: timestamp of the lease first assignment — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

refresh_time.description: timestamp of the last lease refresh — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

host.description: LAN host information from LAN browser (refer to LanHost documentation)

type.description: The valid download types are: — Values: `bt`: bittorrent download; `nzb`: newsgroup download; `http`: HTTP download; `ftp`: FTP download

status.description: The valid download status are: — Values: `stopped`: task is stopped, can be resumed by setting the status to downloading; `queued`: task will start when a new download slot is available the queue position is stored in queue_pos attribute; `starting`: task is preparing to start download; `stopping`: task is gracefully stopping; `error`: there was a problem with the download, you can get an error code in the error field; `done`: the download is over. For bt you can resume seeding setting the status to seeding if the ratio is not reached yet; `checking`: (only valid for nzb) download is over, the downloaded files are being checked using par2; `repairing`: (only valid for nzb) download is over, the downloaded files are being repaired using par2; `extracting`: only valid for nzb) download is over, the downloaded files are being extracted; `seeding`: (only valid for bt) download is over, the content is Change to being shared to other users. The task will automatically stop once the seed ratio has been reached; `retry`: You can set a task status to ‘retry’ to restart the download task.

queue_pos.description: position in download queue (0 if not queued)

io_priority.description: The valid download priorities are: — Values: `low`: low; `normal`: normal; `high`: high

created_ts.description: timestamp of the download creation time — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

download_dir.description: directory where the file(s) will be saved (base64 encoded)

stop_ratio.description: Only relevant for bittorrent tasks. Once the transmit ration has been reached the task will stop seeding.

archive_password.description: ( only relevant for nzb ) password for extracting downloaded archives

info_hash.description: ( only relevant for bt ) torrent info_hash encoded in hexa

piece_length.description: ( only relevant for bt ) torrent piece length in bytes

Download.description: Download objects have the following attributes:

status.description: The valid config status are: — Values: `not_checked`: config has not been checked yet; `checking`: test in progress; `error`: config is invalid, see error; `ok`: config is ok

error.description: The valid config status are: — Values: `none`: test is ok; `nzb_authentication_required`: authentication is required; `bad_authentication`: incorrect credentials; `connection_refused`: unable to connect to NNTP server

enabled_ipv6.description: is the dht enabled on IPv6

node_count_ipv6.description: number of active nodes on IPv6

nb_tasks_active.description: number of active tasks (checking + queued + extracting + repairing + seeding + downloading)

rx_rate.description: current receive rate in bytes / second

tx_rate.description: current transmit rate in bytes / second

throttling_is_scheduled.description: if true, the current throttling mode has been computed using the throttling schedule

filepath.description: full filepath on the disk (encoded as in file system api)

status.description: file download status — Values: `queued`: file is queued for download; `error`: there was a problem with this file, see error to get the error code; `done`: file download is completed

error.description: file error code in case status is error

priority.description: Values: `no_dl`: this file will not be downloaded; `low`: low priority; `normal`: default priority; `high`: high priority

preview_url.description: url to preview downloaded file (only available for bittorrent) as a share link, this url can be use without requiring any form of authentication so that it can be passed as-is to any software.

DownloadFile.description: Each Download has one or more DownloadFile .

is_backup.description: true if the tracker is a backup tracker (the downloader won’t connect to this tracker unless the primary tracker fails)

status.description: tracker status — Values: `unannounced`: not announced; `announcing`: announcing; `announce_failed`: an error occurred while trying to announce; `announced`: announced

interval.description: desired interval between two announces (in seconds)

min_interval.description: minimum interval between two announces (in seconds)

nseeders.description: number of seeders announced on tracker

nleechers.description: number of leechers announced on tracker

DownloadTracker.description: Each tracker is identified by its announce URL.

state.description: peer state — Values: `disconnected`: not connected; `connecting`: trying to connect to the peer; `handshaking`: connected to the peer, negotiating capabilities; `ready`: ready to exchange data

origin.description: peer origin — Values: `tracker`: got the peer from the tracker; `incoming`: incoming peer; `dht`: got the peer from DHT; `pex`: got the peer from Peer exchange protocol; `user`: manually added peer

DownloadPeer.description: Each torrent Download task has one or more DownloadPeer .

reason.description: blacklist reason — Values: `disconnected`: not connected; `connecting`: trying to connect to the peer; `handshaking`: connected to the peer, negotiating capabilities; `ready`: ready to exchange data

global.description: does this entry applies to all torrents

download_dir.description: the default path where downloads will be stored (base64 encoded)

watch_dir.description: special folder that will be monitored. When a new supported file (.nzb, .torrent) is copied in that folder, the task is automatically added to the download queue.

use_watch_dir.description: if set to false, the watch_dir will not be monitored

dns1.description: dns server ip to use for downloader (leave blank for default dns server)

dns2.description: dns server ip to use for downloader

normal.description: download rate for normal time slot (in B/s)

slow.description: download rate for normal slow slot (in B/s)

schedule.description: The schedule array represent the list of week hours timeslot, starting on monday a midnight. Therefore the complete week is represented in a array of 168 elements (24 * 7) — Values: `normal`: downloads will use normal DlRate config for this timeslot; `slow`: downloads will use slow DlRate config for this timeslot; `hibernate`: downloads will be paused for this timeslot

mode.description: Throttling mode can have to following values — Values: `normal`: force use of normal rate limits (not using the scheduler); `slow`: force use of slow rate limits (not using the scheduler); `hibernate`: force hibernate (not using the scheduler); `schedule`: use scheduded rate limit

tx_rate.description: maximum transmit rate (in byte/s) 0 means no limit

rx_rate.description: maximum receive rate (in byte/s) 0 means no limit

ssl.description: Use SSL to connect to server if set to true

user.description: NNTP auth username (can be empty if no auth is required)

password.description: NNTP auth password (can be empty if no auth is required)

nthreads.description: maximum concurrent connections to the NNTP server

auto_repair.description: automatically check and repair downloaded files using the provided par2 files

lazy_par2.description: if set to true the downloader will download the par2 files only if the download is corrupted

auto_extract.description: automatically attempt to extract downloaded files

erase_tmp.description: if auto_extract is enabled, delete archive files once successfully extracted

max_peers.description: maximum number of peers at a given time

stop_ratio.description: default stop_ratio for bt Download tasks

crypto_support.description: The crypto_support can have the following values — Values: `unsupported`: will never use bittorrent crypto; `allowed`: will select plain during handshake; `preferred`: will select crypto during handshake; `required`: will allow plain bittorrent

announce_timeout.description: timeout in seconds for announcing to tracker

fetch_interval.description: interval between automatic RSS refresh (in minutes)

sources[].description: list of block list URL source

status.description: The feed can have the following status — Values: `ready`: feed is up to date; `fetching`: feed is updating; `error`: there was an error trying to refresh this feed, see error

image_url.description: Feed image URL (extracted from the RSS)

nb_read.description: Number of read items in the feed

nb_unread.description: Number of unread items in the feed

auto_download.description: If set to true, the downloader will automatically download new items

fetch_ts.description: Last time the feed was fetched — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

pub_ts.description: Last time the feed was published on remote server — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

error.description: Error code (same as used in Download or DownloadFile ).

DownloadFeed.description: Download Feeds have the following attributes:

link.description: URL of the RSS feed attachment

is_read.description: you can mark the item as read manually, or it is marked as read automatically when the item is downloaded

is_downloaded.description: mark downloaded items, automatically set to true when RSS item is downloaded

fetch_ts.description: timestamp of the item creation — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

pub_ts.description: item publish timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

enclosure_url.description: enclosure URL (if specified in RSS feed)

enclosure_type.description: enclosure mime type (if specified in RSS feed)

enclosure_length.description: enclosure size in bytes (if specified in RSS feed)

DownloadFeedItem.description: Each RSS DownloadFeed contains feed items object

members.description: List of freeplugs member of this network

local.description: if true the Freeplug is connected directly to the Freebox

net_role.description: Values: `sta`: Freeplug Station; `pco`: Freeplug proxy coordinator; `cco`: Central coordinator

eth_port_status.description: Values: `up`: The ethernet port is up; `down`: The ethernet port is down; `unknown`: The ethernet port state is unknown

rx_rate.description: rx rate (from the freeplugs to the “cco” freeplug) (in Mb/s) -1 if not available

tx_rate.description: tx rate (from the “cco” freeplug to the freeplugs) (in Mb/s) -1 if not available

type.description: The valid task types are: — Values: `cat`: Concatenate multiple files; `cp`: Copy files; `mv`: Move files; `rm`: Remove files; `archive`: Creates an archive; `extract`: Extract an archive; `repair`: Check and repair files

state.description: Values: `queued`: Queued (only one task is active at a given time); `running`: Running; `paused`: Paused (user suspended); `done`: Done; `failed`: Failed (see error)

error.description: Values: `none`: No error; `archive_read_failed`: Error reading archive; `archive_open_failed`: Error opening archive; `archive_write_failed`: Error writing archive; `chdir_failed`: Error changing directory; `dest_is_not_dir`: The destination is not a directory; `file_exists`: File already exists; `file_not_found`: File not found; `mkdir_failed`: Unable to create directory; `open_input_failed`: Error opening input file; `open_output_failed`: Error opening output file; `opendir_failed`: Error opening directory; `overwrite_failed`: Error overwriting file; `path_too_big`: Path is too long; `repair_failed`: Failed to repair corrupted files; `rmdir_failed`: Error removing directory; `same_file`: Source and Destination are the same file; `unlink_failed`: Error removing file; `unsupported_file_type`: This file type is not supported; `write_failed`: Error writing file; `disk_full`: Disk is full; `internal`: Internal error; `invalid_format`: Invalid file format (corrupted ?); `incorrect_password`: Invalid or missing password for extraction; `permission_denied`: Permission denied; `readlink_failed`: Failed to read the target of a symbolic link; `symlink_failed`: Failed to create a symbolic link

created_ts.description: task creation timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

started_ts.description: task start timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

done_ts.description: task end timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

eta.description: estimated time remaining before the task completion (in seconds)

curr_bytes.description: size of the file currently processed

curr_bytes_done.description: number of bytes processed for the current file

FsTask.description: File system tasks have the following attributes:

weak_password.description: is the ftp password weak (in this case remote access is disabled)

int_ip.description: the target IP on your LAN

type.description: The valid LAN modes are: — Values: `router`: The Freebox acts as a network router; `bridge`: The Freebox acts as a network bridge

LanConfig.description: Lan config has the following attributes:

primary_name.description: Host primary name (chosen from the list of available names, or manually set by user)

host_type.description: When possible, the Freebox will try to guess the host_type, but you can manually override this to the correct value — Values: `workstation`: Workstation; `laptop`: Laptop; `smartphone`: Smartphone; `tablet`: Tablet; `printer`: Printer; `vg_console`: Video game console; `television`: TV; `nas`: Nas; `ip_camera`: IP Camera; `ip_phone`: IP Phone; `freebox_player`: Freebox Player; `freebox_hd`: Freebox Server; `networking_device`: Networking device; `multimedia_device`: Multimedia device; `other`: Other

l2ident.description: Layer 2 network id and its type

persistent.description: If true the host is always shown even if it has not been active since the Freebox startup

reachable.description: If true the host can receive traffic from the Freebox

last_time_reachable.description: Last time the host was reached — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

active.description: If true the host sends traffic to the Freebox

last_activity.description: Last time the host sent traffic — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

names.description: List of available names, and their source

l3connectivities.description: List of available layer 3 network connections

last_time_reachable.description: last reachable timestamp — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

device_name.description: The name of the device on which the app will be used

TokenRequest.description: Download objects have the following attributes

password.description: The password computed using the challenge and the app_token

DmzConfig.description: Dmz config has the following attributes:

lan_port.description: forwarding target start port on LAN, (last port is lan_port + wan_port_end - wan_port_start)

src_ip.description: if src_ip == 0.0.0.0 this rule will apply to any src ip otherwise it will only apply to the specified ip address

PortForwardingConfig.description: Port forwarding config has the following attributes:

id.description: incoming port id — Values: `http`: http port for remote access to Freebox OS; `https`: https port for tls remote access to Freebox OS; `bittorrent-main`: main bittorrent port for Freebox downloader; `bittorrent-dht`: bittorrent port for DHT; `openvpn_routed`: routed openvpn port; `openvpn_bridge`: bridged openvpn port; `ipsec_ike`: ipsec ikev2 vpn port; `ipsec_nat`: ipsec nat vpn port; `pptp`: pptp vpn server port; `ftp`: ftp control port for FTP remote access; `ftp_pasv`: ftp data port for FTP remote access

active.description: is the port binding currently active

type.description: Values: `tcp`: TCP; `udp`: UDP; `tcp_udp`: both TCP and UDP

netns.description: network namespace. The service may be running on a different namespace (for instance if the service uses the vpn client).

min_port.description: This field indicate the minimum possible value for in_port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

max_port.description: This field indicate the maximum possible value for in_port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

readonly.description: If set to true, the in_port field cannot be changed because of the underlying protocol does not allow it

IncomingPortConfig.description: Incoming port config has the following attributes:

logon_enabled.description: is login/password required to access shares

guest_allow.description: allow guest to access shared files

server_type.description: Afp server type (to display proper icon) in MacOS

ParentalFilterConfig.description: Parental filter configuration allow you to set the default filter_state to apply to hosts without rules on your network

resolution.description: planning resolution : number of planning slots per day, e.g. 48 means that each slot represent 30 minutes

cdayranges.description: list of custom day range, each custom day range represents a group of days for which you want to use a different planning than other week days. — Values: `:fr_bank_holidays`: French bank holidays; `:fr_school_holidays_a`: French school holidays - Zone A; `:fr_school_holidays_b`: French school holidays - Zone B; `:fr_school_holidays_c`: French school holidays - Zone C; `:fr_school_holidays_corse`: French school holidays - Corse

mapping.description: array of states for each corresponding planning slots. the array size is : (number of cdayranges + 7) * resolution

ParentalFilterPlanning.description: Parental filter planning have the following attributes:

macs.description: the list of mac addresses the rule should be applied to

hosts.description: the list of hostnames associated with macs

ip.description: only filled for old rules, you cannot set a rule with an IP

forced.description: is set to true, the rule will not use the planning and will always stay in the forced_mode

forced_mode.description: the filter policy to use when forced is set to true (see filter_state for valid states)

tmp_mode_expire.description: if != 0 the rule will not use the planning for the next tmp_mode_expire seconds

tmp_mode.description: the filter policy to use when tmp_mode_expire is != 0 (see filter_state for valid states)

scheduling_mode.description: Values: `forced`: filter_state is forced to forced_mode; `temporary`: filter_state is temporary set to tmp_mode for the next tmp_mode_expire seconds; `planning`: filter_state is set using the planning

filter_state.description: Values: `allowed`: access is allowed; `denied`: access is denied; `webonly`: access is granted only for HTTP and HTTPS traffic

current_mapping_idx.description: current index used in planning to compute filter_state (only relevant when scheduling_mode is planning)

next_change.description: seconds until next filter_state change 0 means filter_state won’t change

ParentalFilter.description: Parental filter rule objects have the following attributes:

margin_before.description: default margin before recording start time

margin_after.description: default margin after recording end time

altered.description: a precord is altered when some part of the recording may be missing. This can be the case if a conflict occurred during the recording (or connection was down)

state.description: Values: `disabled`: disabled; `start_error`: failed to start; `waiting_start_time`: scheduled; `starting`: starting; `running`: running; `running_error`: running with error; `failed`: failed; `finished`: finished

channel_type.description: Values: `‘’ (empty string)`: auto; `iptv`: use only iptv streams; `dvb`: use only dvb streams

legacy_uri.description: only used for legacy apps. Use channel_uuid instead when available NOTE: only visible when called from player

force_channel_name.description: only used for legacy apps. Use channel_uuid instead when available NOTE: only visible when called from player

media.description: media name on which the record is written. See the Media API for more info. This property and can be empty when the file backing the record is not available, for example when secure is set.

path.description: destination directory on the media storage

byte_size.description: size of the record file in bytes

has_record_gen.description: if true, this frecord has been generated using a Generator

record_gen_id.description: if has_record_gen, this is the id of the generator

altered.description: an frecord is altered when some part of the recording may be missing. This can be the case if a conflict occurred during the recording (or connection was down)

state.description: Values: `disabled`: disabled; `start_error`: failed to start; `waiting_start_time`: scheduled; `starting`: starting; `running`: running; `running_error`: running with error; `failed`: failed; `finished`: finished

channel_type.description: Values: `‘’ (empty string)`: auto; `iptv`: use only iptv streams; `dvb`: use only dvb streams

secure.description: flag set when the record is protected by DRM

free_bytes.description: number of free bytes on the medium

total_bytes.description: total number of bytes on the medium

record_time.description: estimated record time in seconds for multiple channel types and qualities

db.description: Name of the rrd database to read. It can take one of the following values — Values: `net`: network stats; `temp`: temperature stats; `dsl`: xDSL stats; `switch`: switch stats

date_end.description: The requested end timestamp of the stats to get

fields.description: If you are only interested in getting some fields you can provide the list of fields you want to get.

RRDFetch.description: This is the object used to get stats

path.description: The root path of the share, if the path is a regular file, only this file will be shared

name.description: The readable name of the shared file/folder

expire.description: Link expiration timestamp, 0 means no expiration. — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

fullurl.description: Full URL to use for remote access. If remote access is disabled, the field will be empty.

ShareLink.description: Share link have the following attributes:

OperationProgress.description: Operation progress has the following attributes:

state.description: Values: `error`: Partition has error; `checking`: Partition check in progress; `formatting`: Partition format in progress; `mounting`: Partition mount in progress; `maintenance`: Partition is in maintenance mode; `mounted`: Partition is ready; `umounting`: Partition umount in progress; `umounted`: Partition is umounted; `ejecting`: Partition ejection in progress

path.description: partition mount point (encoded in base64 as explained in fs API)

fsck_result.description: fsck result — Values: `no_run_yet`: Partition has not been checked yet; `running`: Check is in progress; `fs_clean`: File system is ok; `fs_corrected`: File system was corrected; `fs_needs_correction`: File system need correction; `failed`: File system has unrecoverable error

DiskPartition.description: Disk partitions have the following attributes:

type.description: Values: `internal`: Freebox internal disk; `usb`: usb disk; `sata`: sata disk

state.description: Values: `error`: Disk has error; `disabled`: Disk is disabled; `enabled`: Disk is enabled; `formatting`: Disk is formatting

time_before_spindown.description: seconds left before disk spin down (in seconds) (when available)

StorageDisk.description: Storage disks have the following attributes:

external_pm_idle_before_spindown.description: idle time in minutes to wait before spinning down an external disk

link.description: Values: `up`: port is up; `down`: port is down

duplex.description: Values: `half`: force in half duplex mode; `full`: force in full duplex mode

mode.description: display form of speed and duplex mode

mac_list.description: list of { mac, name } of hosts connected to this port

duplex.description: Values: `auto`: auto negotiate duplex mode; `half`: force in half duplex mode; `full`: force in full duplex mode

disk_status.description: the internal disk status — Values: `not_detected`: The disk as not been detected; `disabled`: The disk is disabled; `initializing`: The disk is initializing; `error`: The disk failed to mount; `active`: The disk is ready

box_flavor.description: the box ‘flavor’ for a given model — Values: `full`: The box has an internal storage; `light`: The box has no internal storage

user_main_storage.description: The label of the storage partition to use for user data. (Matches the label of the DiskPartition ) In case of ‘light’ box flavor, it must be set by to a permanently attached external storage

status.description: upload status can have the following values — Values: `authorized`: Upload authorization is valid, upload has not started yet; `in_progress`: Upload in progress; `done`: Upload done; `failed`: Upload failed; `conflict`: Destination file conflict; `timeout`: Upload authorization is no longer valid; `cancelled`: Upload cancelled by user

start_date.description: upload start date — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

last_update.description: last update of file upload object — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch).

FileUpload.description: File uploads have the following attributes:

force.description: select the way conflicts are handled — Values: `missing`: The response to the FileUploadStartAction will be an error with ‘destination_conflict’ if the destination file already exists. The response will also contain a file_size attribute containing the existing file length (useful for resuming upload); `overwrite`: If the target file already exists it will be overridden; `resume`: The upload will resume, all sent chunks will then be appended to the existing file.

complete.description: will be true in a reply to FileUploadFinalizeAction or FileUploadCancelAction

cancelled.description: will be true in a reply FileUploadCancelAction

FileUploadChunkResponse.description: For each received chunk, the Freebox will send a chunk response containing upload progress information the request_id used in response will be the one from the FileUploadStartAction , and ‘action’ value will be ‘upload_data’

enabled.description: is the UPnP AV service enabled

type.description: VPN server type — Values: `pptp`: PPTP VPN server; `openvpn`: OpenVPN server

auth_connection_count.description: number of active connections that have passed authentication

mppe.description: Values: `disable`: disable mppe; `require`: require mppe; `require_128`: require 128 bits mppe

allowed_auth.description: allowed authentication methods dictionnary with following entries:

id_custom.description: value of the source id when id_source is custom

auth_modes.description: map of supported auth modes, currently only psk is supported

type.description: VPN server type — Values: `pptp`: PPTP VPN server; `openvpn`: OpenVPN server; `ipsec`: IPsec IKEv2 server

min_port.description: This field indicate the minimum possible value for port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

max_port.description: This field indicate the maximum possible value for port (see ConnectionStatus ipv4_port_range)

conf_pptp.description: only available when type is PPTP

conf_openvpn.description: only available when type is OpenVPN

conf_ipsec.description: only available when type is IPsec

ip_start.description: start of the IP range that will be used to give clients an IP

ip_end.description: end of the IP range that will be used to give clients an IP

ip6_start.description: start of the IPv6 range that will be used to give clients an IPv6

ip6_end.description: end of the IPv6 range that will be used to give clients an IPv6

password.description: VPN user password (length must be between 8 and 32)

password_set.description: True if a password was provided for this user

ip_reservation.description: You can specify the IP you want to assign to this user. If you don’t want to use a specific IP pass an empty string or omit this property.

local_ip.description: attributed IP address from VPN adress pool

type.description: VPN server type — Values: `pptp`: PPTP VPN server; `openvpn`: OpenVPN server

active.description: is this configuration active. Only one configuration is active at a time.

conf_pptp.description: only available when type is PPTP

mppe.description: Values: `disable`: disable mppe; `require`: require mppe; `require_128`: require 128 bits mppe

allowed_auth.description: allowed authentication methods dictionnary with following keys:

type.description: active VPN type — Values: `pptp`: PPTP VPN server; `openvpn`: OpenVPN server

state.description: Values: `waiting_wan`: waiting for wan connection; `going_up`: connecting; `up`: connected; `going_down`: disconnecting; `down`: disconnected

next_try.description: seconds left until next connection attempt

last_error.description: Values: `none`: no error; `internal`: internal error; `authentication_failed`: wrong credentials; `auth_failed`: wrong credentials; `resolv_failed`: invalid host name; `connect_timeout`: connection timeout; `connect_failed`: connection failed; `setup_control_failed`: PPTP session negociation failure; `setup_call_failed`: PPTP session failure; `protocol`: protocol error; `remote_terminated`: connection closed by remote peer; `remote_disconnect`: connection closed by remote peer

provider.description: ip_mask source — Values: `none`: none; `static`: static IP configuration; `ppp`: ppp; `dhcp`: DHCP server

mac_filter_state.description: Values: `disabled`: mac filter is disabled; `whitelist`: mac filter is enabled, using a whitelist; `blacklist`: mac filter is enabled, using a blacklist

WifiAp.description: The Freebox may have one or more access points, you can configure each access point with this api.

state.description: Values: `scanning`: Ap is probing wifi channels; `no_param`: Ap is not configured; `bad_param`: Ap has an invalid configuration; `disabled`: Ap is permanently disabled; `disabled_planning`: Ap is currently disabled according to planning; `no_active_bss`: Ap has no active BSS; `starting`: Ap is starting; `acs`: Ap is selecting the best available channel; `ht_scan`: Ap is scanning for other access point; `dfs`: Ap is performing dynamic frequency selection; `active`: Ap is active; `failed`: Ap has failed to start

channel_width.description: effective channel width (in MHz) primary_channel int Read-only ¶ effective primary channel secondary_channel int Read-only ¶ effective secondary channel dfs_cac_remaining_time int Read-only ¶ time left in dfs state

WifiApStatus.description: The Freebox may have one or more access points, you can configure each access point with this api.

2d4g.description: map of capabilites in 2.4 GHz band

5g.description: map of capabilites in 5 GHz band

60g.description: map of capabilites in 60 GHz band

WifiApCapabilities.description: The Freebox may have one or more access points, you can configure each access point with this api.

WifiApHtConfig.description: NOTE: before enabling some feature in ap config, you should ensure that AP supports the feature using its provided capabilities.

primary_channel.description: wanted primary channel, value of 0 means automatic selection

secondary_channel.description: wanted secondary channel, value of 0 means automatic selection

WifiApConfig.description: NOTE: before enabling some feature in ap config, you should ensure that AP supports the feature using its provided capabilities.

band.description: the band for which the combination can be used — Values: `2d4g`: 2.4 GHz; `5g`: 5 GHz; `60g`: 60 GHz

channel_width.description: the channel_width for which the combination can be used

dfs_cac_time.description: time required in dfs state before behing able to start the AP.

secondary.description: secondary channel (zero means that secondary channel will not be used)

WifiAllowedComb.description: To be able to allow user to pick a valid channel combination for a given AP you should use the following api to retreive the list of allowed channel combination.

bssid.description: bssid on which the client is associated

state.description: Values: `associated`: station is associated; `authenticated`: station is authenticated

bitrate.description: physical link rate (in 1/10th of MBit/s), -1 if unknown

mcs.description: current link mcs, -1 if not used

vht_mcs.description: current link vht mcs, -1 if not used

state.description: Values: `phy_stopped`: associated AP is stopped; `no_param`: bss is missing config; `bad_param`: bss has an invalid config; `disabled`: bss is disabled; `starting`: bss is starting; `active`: bss is active; `failed`: bss has failed to start

sta_count.description: number of stations for this bss authorized_sta_count int Read-only ¶ number of authenticated stations for this bss is_main_bss bool Read-only ¶ if bss is main bs, its configuration will be reused for other bss with attribute use_default_config set as true. This can be used to apply the same configuration to 2 bss on 2 AP operating on different wifi bands.

authorized_sta_count.description: number of authenticated stations for this bss

is_main_bss.description: if bss is main bs, its configuration will be reused for other bss with attribute use_default_config set as true. This can be used to apply the same configuration to 2 bss on 2 AP operating on different wifi bands.

enabled.description: enable this BSS. Note that if you want the AP to completely stop emitting wifi you should use WifiGlobalConfig enabled attribute, otherwise FreeWifi and FreeWifi Secure may still be active.

use_default_config.description: Values: `wep`: wep (should not use); `wpa_psk_auto`: wpa/psk auto; `wpa_psk_tkip`: wpa/psk tkip; `wpa_psk_ccmp`: wpa/psk ccmp; `wpa2_psk_auto`: wpa2/psk auto; `wpa2_psk_tkip`: wpa2/psk tkip; `wpa2_psk_ccmp`: wpa2/psk ccmp

hide_ssid.description: don’t show bss in bss list

encryption.description: Values: `wep`: wep (should not use); `wpa_psk_auto`: wpa/psk auto; `wpa_psk_tkip`: wpa/psk tkip; `wpa_psk_ccmp`: wpa/psk ccmp; `wpa2_psk_auto`: wpa2/psk auto; `wpa2_psk_tkip`: wpa2/psk tkip; `wpa2_psk_ccmp`: wpa2/psk ccmp

band.description: the band for which the combination can be used — Values: `2d4g`: 2.4 GHz; `5g`: 5 GHz; `60g`: 60 GHz

noise_level.description: noise level on channel in dB

resolution.description: planning resolution (number of slots per day)

mapping.description: mapping for planning : “on” or “off” mapping[0] is monday at 0:0 mapping[7 * resolution - 1] is sunday last slot

type.description: Values: `whitelist`: if mac_filter is set to whitelist this station will be allowed; `blacklist`: if mac_filter is set to blacklist this station will be rejected
